seo.pageTitle: Jeju Southwest Hydrangea Festival Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: Summer-only southwest Jeju day tour: Hyeopjae Beach, Hallim Park hydrangea festival, Jusangjeolli Cliff, Cheonjeyeon Falls, Camellia Hill, Osulloc tea fields.
seo.title: 제주 서남부 수국 축제 소그룹 투어 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 제주 서남부 여름 수국 투어(5월 말~7월): 협재해변 → 한림공원 수국 축제 → 주상절리+천제연폭포 → 카멜리아힐 수국 터널 → 오설록 차밭.

catalog_card.title: 제주 서남부 수국 축제 소그룹 투어
catalog_card.subtitle: 협재해변, 한림공원 수국 축제, 주상절리·천제연폭포, 카멜리아힐 수국 터널, 오설록 차밭을 소그룹으로.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 협재해변, 한림공원 수국 축제(개방형 수국 정원), 주상절리(유네스코)·천제연폭포(묶음 관람), 카멜리아힐 수국 터널, 오설록 티뮤지엄을 소그룹으로 여름 하루에.

itineraryStops.name: 협재해수욕장
itineraryStops.category: West-coast emerald beach + Biyangdo Island view
itineraryStops.description: 제주 서부 대표 해수욕장. 에메랄드빛 바다와 비양도 풍경. 방문 시간: 약 40분.
highlights: **Top 3 west-coast Jeju beaches / Top 10 overall** — emerald-turquoise water + silvery sand
**Distinctive silvery-white sand** — crushed seashells + coral fragments
**9 km shoreline** when paired with twin-beach **Geumneung Beach** (less crowded, walkable)
**Biyangdo Island** — 1.2 km offshore, ~200 residents, **Korea's first carbon-free island certification**
**Sunset behind Biyangdo** — catalog's premier west-coast sunset framing
**Pine forest (Songlim) backdrop** with free camping ground
**Tidal pools at low tide** — hermit crabs, shrimp, juvenile fish; Biyangdo 'walkable' optical effect
**Lifeguarded swim period July 1 – August 31**; wheelchair-accessible ramp at multiple points
**Most-developed west-coast café strip** behind the beach
timeUsed: Main beach walk + photo windows — ~20 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **West-coast beach opener — and the natural pre-anchor for Hallim Park.** Three southwest-route reasons Hyeopjae is the morning opener at 09:30. (1) **Geographic pairing with Hallim**: Hyeopjae Beach sits **a 5-minute walk north of Hallim Park's main gate** — pairing them as a single morning duo is the local-operator standard and converts what would otherwise be 'two stops within 1 km' into a coherent Hyeopjae→Hallim arc. Visiting Hyeopjae first preserves a **clean, uncrowded beach morning** before the 10:30 Hallim gate-opening tour-bus rush. (2) **Light timing**: Hyeopjae's signature emerald-turquoise water + silvery-white seashell-sand contrast is most photogenic in the **9:30–10:30 morning window** when the sun is high enough to saturate the colors but the late-morning haze hasn't yet built up off the shoreline. (3) **Biyangdo Island anchor for the day's signature wide-landscape photo**: Biyangdo (1.2 km offshore, ~200 residents) sits as the day's first and only **horizon-island silhouette** — every subsequent stop is inland or coastal-cliff, so locking the Biyangdo wide-shot as the morning's first deliverable gives the day's photo set its required landscape breadth before pivoting into the garden + columnar-cliff + tea-fields close.

sources: VisitJeju — Hyeopjae Beach + Biyangdo Island official listings
Jeju Special Self-Governing Province — Top Jeju beaches ranking + amenity registry
Korea Maritime + Ports Office — Hallim Port ferry schedule to Biyangdo
Jeju Tourism Organization — west-coast sunset photography spots registry
Hallim-eup local cultural office — Geumneung twin-beach pairing documentation

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours; managed swim season July–August

smartNotes.photo: Sunset shots with Biyangdo silhouette; turquoise color is most vivid at midday under direct sun
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along beachfront; convenience stores
smartNotes.tip: Combine with Hallim Park (5 min walk); avoid summer weekends for fewer crowds

itineraryStops.name: 한림공원 (수국 축제)
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea anchor + 9 themed gardens + 2 lava-tube caves
itineraryStops.description: 1971년 개장한 약 9만9,000㎡ 규모의 식물원으로, 수국 시즌(6~7월)에는 100여 종의 수국 품종이 공원 전체에 개화합니다. 쌍용굴·협재굴 용암동굴도 포함되어 있습니다. 입장료 별도(약 ₩15,000). 방문 시간: 약 70분.
highlights: **Founded 1971** by Song Bong-gyu — sandy wasteland transformed into a green paradise
**~99,000 m² / 80 acres** — 9 themed gardens + ~1.2 million visitors per year
**June Hydrangea Festival** — the park's signature June anchor
**Monthly festival calendar** — named active festival every month of the year
**Hyeopjaegul Cave** (~200m × 10m × 5m, ~25 million years old) — lava-tube + limestone stalactites
**Ssangyonggul Cave** ('Two Dragons', ~400m × 6m × 3m) — branching paths cave system
**'Two-dimensional caves'** — only lava-tube + limestone caves in the world (17 km system)
Cave temp **17–18°C year-round** — natural summer refuge
Diplomatic guest list: **Jiang Zemin, Nakasone, Kim Dae-jung, Lee Kun-hee, Chung Ju-yung**
timeUsed: Subtropical / Palm / Wild Flower gardens — 30 min
Hydrangea garden + Jae-Am Folk Village — 35 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's hydrangea anchor — and the southwest route's identity-defining stop.** Four reasons Hallim is the late-morning anchor at 10:30 with a generous 120-min dwell. (1) **Hydrangea season alignment**: June is the Hallim Park monthly-calendar **Hydrangea Festival month** — Hallim is the first of the day's three hydrangea-coded venues (Hallim morning anchor → Camellia Hill afternoon hydrangea tunnel → Osulloc tea-garden afternoon green tone). (2) **Cave-as-weather-fallback**: the **Hyeopjaegul + Ssangyonggul cave pairing** gives the day a guaranteed **indoor-equivalent refuge** against unpredictable southwest-Jeju June weather (June is Korea's pre-monsoon shoulder; afternoon thundershowers happen ~1 in 4 days). The 17–18°C cave temperature also serves as the day's **mid-morning cool-down** before the warm midday transit. (3) **120-min dwell justification**: Hallim's 9 themed gardens + 2 caves cannot be done meaningfully in less than 90 minutes — the 120-min slot accounts for **slow-walking in the caves** (uneven lava-tube floors), **photo dwell at hydrangea field**, and **typical 5-min queue at the cave entrance** during peak season. (4) **Operational pairing with Hyeopjae**: because Hyeopjae sits 5 min walk away, the morning Hyeopjae→Hallim arc consumes 160 min total but only 5 min of vehicle transit — extracting maximum guest value from the operator's morning fuel budget.

sources: http://jejucvb.com/Hallim — Jeju CVB official Unique Venue page (founder Song Bong-gyu + diplomatic guest list)
https://www.tripadvisor.com/Attraction_Review-g297885-d2204895-Reviews-Hallim_Park-Jeju_Jeju_Island.html — Tripadvisor Hallim Park (annual visitors + 9 themed gardens + monthly festival calendar)
https://www.trazy.com/experience/detail/hallim-park-jeju-ticket — Trazy Hallim Park ticket page (cave system + 'two-dimensional caves' classification + Natural Monument designation)
https://www.expatolife.com/hallim-park-jeju-island-south-korea/ — Expatolife Hallim Park guide (cave dimensions + 17 km lava-tube system)

visitBasics.hours: Mar–May 09:00–18:30; Jun–Aug 09:00–19:00; Sep–Oct 09:00–18:30; Nov–Feb 09:00–17:30 (last admission 1 hour before close)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 15,000 KRW / Teen 10,000 KRW / Child 9,000 KRW (online discount: ≈13,500 / 9,000 / 8,100)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 2–3 hours of walking through 9 garden zones; mostly flat

smartNotes.photo: Hydrangea Festival (June) and pink muhly (October) are peak photo seasons; lava caves with stalactite lighting are unique
smartNotes.facilities: On-site restaurants, gift shop, cave museum
smartNotes.tip: Last entry 90 minutes before closing; closing time depends on sunset; pair with Hyeopjae Beach (5–10 min walk)

itineraryStops.name: 점심 (제주 서남부 현지 식당)
itineraryStops.description: 한림·애월·안덕 지역 현지 식당. 점심 식사비 별도(약 ₩12,000~18,000).
highlights: **Southwest-Jeju local restaurant** — Hallim / Aewol-tail / Andeok corridor
Vetted shortlist: **English-friendly menus** + dietary accommodation
Signature options: **galchi-jorim, heuk-dwaeji bulgogi, mom-guk (regional), bibimbap**
**Mom-guk** = west-Jeju regional specialty (seaweed-pork soup) not on east tours
**Lunch at guests' own expense** (~₩12,000–₩18,000/person, not included)
Vegetarian + halal-friendly options on request
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Operational midday stop**, locked at 12:35 to bridge the morning Hyeopjae+Hallim anchor (120 min Hallim runs into early-lunch zone) and the afternoon Jusangjeolli/Cheonjeyeon + Camellia Hill arc. The 60-min slot also serves as a **buffer absorber** — if the Hallim cave queue runs long during peak-season weekends, the lunch window compresses to 50 min without disrupting the 13:50 columnar-cliff target.

visitBasics.admission: Lunch cost paid direct, ≈10,000–15,000 KRW typical (Korean local restaurants near route)

smartNotes.tip: Vegetarian options on request — notify the day before.

itineraryStops.name: 주상절리 & 천제연폭포
itineraryStops.category: Compound stop — Natural Monument 443 columnar joints + Natural Monument 378 three-tier waterfall
itineraryStops.description: 대포 주상절리(유네스코 지질공원 현무암 절벽)와 천제연폭포(3단 폭포)를 75분 합산 도보 코스로 묶어 관람합니다. 입장료 별도(주상절리 ₩2,000, 천제연 ₩2,500). 방문 시간: 약 75분.
highlights: **Compound stop** — 2 Natural Monuments paired in one 75-min walk (3 km / 7-min drive between)
**Daepo Jusangjeolli — Natural Monument No. 443 (2005)** — most recent Jeju coastal NM
**1.75 km coastal cliff** of hexagonal/pentagonal black basalt columns up to 30–40m tall
Part of **Jeju UNESCO Global Geopark (2010)** — formed ~140,000–250,000 years ago
**Wave heights up to 20m+** during peak south swells crashing against columns
**Cheonjeyeon Falls — Natural Monument No. 378 (1993)** — three-tier waterfall + transition forest
**Seven Nymphs (선녀) Joseon Daoist legend** — heavenly nymphs descended to bathe nightly
**Seonimgyo Bridge (선임교)** — 128-m arched footbridge with 7 nymph statues
**Floral counterpoint** — the day's only non-garden geological + waterfall stop
timeUsed: Jusangjeolli cliff observation deck + photos — 25 min
Drive to Cheonjeyeon (~7 min) + restroom — 12 min
Cheonjeyeon Seonimgyo Bridge + upper-pond viewpoint — 25 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's geological + waterfall counterpoint to the three-garden floral arc.** Three southwest-route reasons this compound stop sits at 13:50. (1) **Geographic necessity**: Jusangjeolli + Cheonjeyeon are both in **Jungmun (~3 km apart)** — either both go in or both stay out. Going in is necessary because they are the **only Natural Monuments on the day's path** (alongside the Black Pig at Hueree on the sister tour) and provide the day's required UNESCO-Geopark anchor. (2) **Pacing counterweight**: without this stop, the southwest tour collapses into a garden-only sequence (Hallim hydrangea → Camellia Hill hydrangea → Osulloc tea) that loses visual variety after stop 2. The columnar cliff seascape + three-tier waterfall gorge give the afternoon the visual palette breadth that makes it a complete 'southwest Jeju day' rather than a flower-only tour. (3) **Compound efficiency**: pairing both as 75-min stop frees up **45+ min** vs. visiting each as separate ~60-min stops — that surplus is what funds Hallim's 120-min anchor dwell + Camellia Hill's afternoon photo time.

_poi_meta.compound_stop_note: Paired stop — Jusangjeolli + Cheonjeyeon visited within a single 75-min transit-shared window. Primary KB anchor `daepo_jusangjeolli_cliff` (facts in poi_knowledge_base_v1.21.json), secondary `cheonjeyeon_falls`.
sources: https://heritage.unesco.or.kr/jeju-volcanic-island-and-lava-tubes/ — Korea National Heritage Service Jeju Volcanic listing
https://www.peek.com/seogwipo-si-jeju-do-south-korea/r0an9mv/jeju-island-hydrangeas-a-floral-paradise/a067e9nx — Peek 'Jeju Island Hydrangeas' tour itinerary documenting the compound-stop pattern

visitBasics.admission: Jusangjeolli: Adult 2,000 / Child 1,000. Cheonjeyeon: Adult 2,500 / Child 1,350. Combined ≈4,500 KRW per adult.
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat wooden boardwalk along cliff; total walk 15–20 min

smartNotes.photo: Best with high tide and rough seas — waves smashing into the columns make dramatic shots; golden hour gives best column-color contrast
smartNotes.tip: Get there before 11am or after 4pm to avoid tour-bus crowds; combine with Jungmun Saekdal Beach (5 min) for a half-day
smartNotes.facilities: Small geological exhibition near entrance; souvenir shop

itineraryStops.name: 카멜리아힐 (수국 터널)
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea tunnel + 172,000 m² largest camellia arboretum in the East
itineraryStops.description: 카멜리아힐 172,000㎡ 식물원의 수국 터널 — 좁은 산책로 양쪽을 수국이 둘러싸는 독특한 터널 구성. 한림공원 개방형 수국 정원과 대조되는 구성미. 입장료 별도(약 ₩10,000). 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **172,000 m²** — largest camellia arboretum in the East
**6,000 trees / 500+ species from 80 countries** — accumulated 30+ years
**Year-round bloom calendar** — Nov–Mar camellias, Mar–May cherry + tulip, Jun–Aug hydrangea
**Themed sections**: European Garden, Korean Native Forest, Cherry Walk, Hydrangea Field
Glass **greenhouse + gallery + cafe** complex — indoor refuge (15% of visit)
**'EXchange / Transit Love 3'** filming location — Ju-won & Yu-jeong's date scene
**Open 365 days**; closes at sundown (no outdoor lighting)
Admission **₩12,000 adult / ₩10,000 youth+senior / ₩9,000 child (4–11) / FREE under 3**
**~85% outdoor** garden walking — comfortable shoes recommended
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The afternoon's hydrangea-tunnel anchor — and the day's photographic centerpiece.** Three southwest-route reasons Camellia Hill is the afternoon-2 anchor at 15:15. (1) **Hydrangea Tunnel — Korea's earliest blooming hydrangeas**: Camellia Hill houses **Korea's earliest-blooming hydrangeas (early April indoor + outdoor full bloom from June)**, with the **Hydrangea Hall pergola tunnel** as the day's definitive Instagram-anchor photo (giant blue/pink/purple hydrangea balls hanging above a covered walkway). The June 10–25 peak window aligns exactly with this tour's seasonal positioning. (2) **Garden-pacing complementarity**: Camellia Hill's **1.2 km / 0.75-mile walking loop** is gentler than Hallim's 9-themed-garden complexity, making it the day's **photo-immersive low-effort garden** after the morning's higher-density Hallim anchor. The two gardens together build a **garden-pacing arc** (intensive Hallim → relaxed Camellia Hill) that wouldn't work if reversed. (3) **Late-afternoon light**: the 15:15–16:30 window catches the **warm late-afternoon golden light** that flatters the hydrangea pinks and blues (midday flat light desaturates them, same logic as Hueree on the east route). Camellia Hill's signature photographers'-mirror installation also captures golden-hour reflections from this slot. **Sister-route note**: this stop matches the afternoon timing of Hueree on the east route — both sister tours close their hydrangea anchor in late afternoon for matching sun angles.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization — Top Korean botanical gardens registry
Kakao TV / Wavve programming archive — 'EXchange / Transit Love 3' filming locations

visitBasics.admission: Adult 10,000 KRW / Teen 8,000 KRW / Child 7,000 KRW (approximate)
visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — gentle hill paths; mostly stroller-accessible

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance and within garden

smartNotes.photo: Hydrangea tunnel in June; pink muhly grass field in October; camellia varieties peak Nov–Apr
smartNotes.tip: Visit before 10am or after 4pm in peak season to avoid crowds; pair with Osulloc (10 min drive)
smartNotes.facilities: Indoor greenhouse café, gift shop, picnic areas

itineraryStops.name: 오설록 티뮤지엄
itineraryStops.category: Amorepacific tea-culture flagship + green-tea fields finale
itineraryStops.description: 아모레퍼시픽 운영 한국 최대 녹차 재배지 문화 공간. 녹차밭 전망, 음료·아이스크림 구매 가능. 입장 무료. 방문 시간: 약 40분.
highlights: **Amorepacific** flagship — Korea's largest beauty company; founder Suh Sung-whan pioneered Jeju tea **1979**
**Seogwang Dawon = Korea's largest green-tea field**; ~700 tonnes annual production
Museum **opened 2001** — designboom Top 10 art museums (architecture)
**FREE admission**, daily **09:00–18:00**, ~2 million annual visitors
Antique tea-ware collection spanning **Tang dynasty (618–907 CE)** to modern Korean Joseon ceramics
**Green tea rolling cake** + **green tea ice cream** = signature cafe items
**Innisfree Jeju House** 5-min walk — free, optional tangerine soap DIY ₩15,000
Plantation walking paths **best at golden hour (16:00–17:00)** for photography
Rooftop deck + ground-level rows give two distinct elevations for tea-row composition
timeUsed: Tea House cafe (rolling cake + ice cream) — ~20 min
Optional Innisfree Jeju House visit — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's tea-culture finale — and the southwest route's signature non-floral close.** Three reasons Osulloc closes the day at 16:45. (1) **Tea-color counterpoint to the day's hydrangea palette**: after 4 garden/cliff stops in varying flower-heavy palettes, Osulloc's **emerald-green tea-field rows + Innisfree Jeju House's white-glass architecture** give the day's photo set its closing color tone — saturated green that contrasts with the morning's hydrangea blues/pinks. (2) **Indoor-finale + restroom-buffer**: Osulloc's tea museum + cafe provide the day's **only indoor late-afternoon stop** — guests can use the museum's tea-tasting + ice-cream + restroom facilities to decompress before the **40-min westbound transit back to Jeju City**. The 60-min dwell is sized exactly to allow tea purchase + ice cream + 1 quick tea-field walk. (3) **Brand-cultural anchor**: Osulloc as the **Amorepacific tea flagship (founded 1979 by founder Suh Sung-whan)** ties the day's tour back to one of Korea's globally-recognized brands — a deliberate brand-cultural close that distinguishes this route from generic 'flower garden tours' and gives the day a clear cultural-economic finale story.

sources: designboom Top 10 art museums archival listing
Korea Tea Industry Association — production statistics

visitBasics.admission: Free for museum; tea/desserts in café paid; Innisfree workshops paid separately
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat museum and stroller-friendly tea fields; well-maintained paths

smartNotes.photo: Tea fields with tour-cup shaped museum building; outdoor seating area; cherry blossoms peak shoulder season
smartNotes.tip: Arrive before 10:30 to beat the tour-bus rush; allow 1.5–2 hours including café time and tea field walk
smartNotes.facilities: Café (must-try green tea ice cream), gift shop, Innisfree Jeju House next door, observatory deck overlooking fields

routePhases.description: 한림·애월·안덕 인근 제주 서남부 현지 식당.
주상절리+천제연폭포 → 카멜리아힐 수국 터널 → 오설록 차밭.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this summer day moves
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Beach warm-up, hydrangea festival, lunch reset, then UNESCO geology, hydrangea tunnel, and tea fields. 08:30 to 17:30.

items.label: Beach as Light Opener
items.detail: Hyeopjae as a low-intensity start lets the group settle before higher-attention bloom and geology sites.

items.label: Two Hydrangea Stops, Different Formats
items.detail: Hallim festival garden and Camellia tunnel give complementary compositions of the same flower — bookend morning and afternoon.

items.label: Tea to Close
items.detail: Osulloc closes calmly — culture and rest after the visual intensity of bloom and falls.

items.label: Hydrangea Window Late May - mid-July
items.detail: Hydrangea blooms appear from late May, peak mid-June, and fade by mid-July. Outside this window the bloom stops lose their visual draw.

items.label: Outside Window, Pick SW-Small
items.detail: If your dates fall outside hydrangea season, our West & South year-round route covers the same region with year-round POIs (Hallasan, Osulloc, Aewol).

items.label: Camellia Hill is Not Camellia Season
items.detail: Despite the name, this tour visits Camellia Hill specifically for hydrangea — actual camellias bloom in winter.

items.label: 9 Hours Is Long
items.detail: Six diverse stops in 9 hours — pace is fuller than east-small. Build energy with steady hydration.

items.label: Some Stops Have Admission
items.detail: Hallim Park (15,000 KRW) and Camellia Hill (10,000 KRW) admissions are paid on-site, not bundled into the tour price. Budget ≈30,000 KRW per person across all admissions (Jusangjeolli 2,000 + Cheonjeyeon 2,500 round it out).

items.label: Heat Can Be Real
items.detail: June Jeju can hit 30°C+ at midday. Bring sun protection — most stops are outdoor.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hotel lobby pickup in Jeju City area
content: Pickup from your hotel lobby between 08:00-08:30
Return drop-off by approximately 17:30-18:00
Hotel lobby pickup within Jeju City is free. Pickup from Seogwipo, Aewol, or Seongsan adds 70,000 KRW due to extra driving distance.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
content: Comfortable walking shoes (multiple garden walks)
Sun protection — hat, sunscreen, sunglasses
Light layer for AC vehicle and tea house
Cash or card for lunch and admission

practicalAccordionItems.title: Hydrangea season window
content: Tour season: June 1 - July 15 (varies ±5 days year to year)
Peak window: June 25 - July 5 (best photographic conditions)
We update the booking page with year-specific bloom forecast in early May
If your travel dates fall outside the window, consider our West & South year-round route instead

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather policy
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Light rain operates; heavy rain reschedules
content: Light rain: tour operates (hydrangea actually photographs well in soft rain)
Heavy rain: free rescheduling within season window
Outside-window cancellations follow the booking platform's standard terms — see the booking confirmation for specifics.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
content: Hotel pickup and drop-off (Jeju City)
Air-conditioned van transport
Licensed English-speaking guide
Major admissions (Hallim Park, Camellia Hill, Jusangjeolli, Cheonjeyeon) are paid on-site, not bundled into the tour price
Lunch separate from base price

bookingSupportSteps.description: 출발 전날 저녁 최종 픽업 시각 확인.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 당일 코스 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Driver checks in 10 minutes before arrival
bookingSupportSteps.description: 날씨 및 개화 상태 아침 메시지 전달.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 경유지별 심층 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Direct line to operations if anything comes up
bookingSupportSteps.description: 수국 품종, 주상절리 생성 원리 등 가이드 설명.

staticQuestions.question: 제주를 처음 방문하는 분에게 좋은가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 수국 시즌 방문 시 네. 그 외 시기에는 연중 운행 서부·남부 투어를 추천합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 수국 시즌은 정확히 언제인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 약 5월 말~7월 중순(절정 6월 중순). 매년 1~2주 변동. 예약 시 최신 정보를 안내합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 비가 오면 어떻게 되나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 가벼운 비: 진행합니다. 수국은 부드러운 빗속에서 더 아름답게 촬영됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 왜 수국 경유지가 두 곳인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 한림공원은 개방형 축제 정원, 카멜리아힐은 터널 형식입니다. 같은 꽃이지만 전혀 다른 촬영 구도와 분위기를 연출합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어린이와 함께할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 만 6세 이상 추천. 대부분 경유지 도보가 보통 수준입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어르신도 참여할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 대부분 쉬운 도보. 천제연 폭포 계단은 건너뛰고 주상절리만 관람하면 됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 픽업 장소는 어디인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 제주시 호텔 로비 무료 픽업. 서귀포·애월·성산 픽업은 추가 비용(₩70,000).

staticQuestions.question: 입장료가 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 기본 포함되지 않습니다. 하루 약 ₩30,000: 한림공원 ₩15,000, 카멜리아힐 ₩10,000, 주상절리 ₩2,000, 천제연 ₩2,500. 오설록 무료.

staticQuestions.question: 오설록이 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 오설록 티뮤지엄은 무료 입장입니다. 다실 음료는 별도 구매.

staticQuestions.question: 연중 서부·남부 투어와 어떻게 다른가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 이 투어는 수국 시즌 한정으로 한림공원·카멜리아힐 수국이 추가됩니다. 연중 투어는 한라산 1100고지·송악산·오설록을 포함합니다.

walking_notes: Six stops with mostly flat garden walking.
Sun exposure is real in midsummer — bring sun protection.
Total walking moderate; longest single walk is Hallim Park hydrangea zones.

matching_metadata.source_of_truth_note: matching_profile fields scored against atockorea matching algorithm v2. Single fixed route; no variant overrides.
matching_metadata.audit_summary: Southwest-Jeju Hydrangea Festival route — 9-hr small-group minicoach loop. 5 substantive POI anchors + 1 compound-stop pairing: 1 west-coast emerald beach (Hyeopjae) + 1 120-min hydrangea festival anchor (Hallim Park, June Hydrangea Festival, 9 themed gardens + 2 lava-tube limestone caves) + 1 compound stop (Jusangjeolli columnar + Cheonjeyeon waterfall, Natural Monuments 443 + 378) + 1 hydrangea tunnel anchor (Camellia Hill — 172,000 m², Korea's earliest hydrangeas) + 1 tea-culture finale (Osulloc / Amorepacific). Designed for **June peak hydrangea window (June 10–25)** with Hallim caves + Camellia Hall + Osulloc museum as the 3-tier indoor weather-fallback stack. **Catalog-novel pattern**: Jusangjeolli+Cheonjeyeon compound stop is the first of its kind in the catalog. Cross-batch reuse: 3 of 5 substantive bodies imported verbatim (15a T2 Hyeopjae, 15a T1 Camellia Hill, 14a T2 Osulloc); Hallim Park authored fresh + compound stop authored fresh.

high_scores.southwest_jeju_fit=1.0: All 5 substantive stops are on the southwest corridor (Hallim, Jungmun, Andeok, Seogwang)
high_scores.hydrangea_festival_anchor_count=3: Hallim June Hydrangea + Camellia Hill hydrangea tunnel + Osulloc tea-garden green tone
high_scores.hydrangea_tunnel_visit=True: Unique to this route — Camellia Hill's signature pergola tunnel
high_scores.two_dimensional_caves_fit=1.0: Hallim's Hyeopjaegul + Ssangyonggul are uniquely classified as the only lava-tube + limestone caves in the world
high_scores.honeymoon_fit=1.0: Camellia Hill is one of Korea's top wedding-snap photo destinations + lower-impact pacing
high_scores.compound_stop_count=1: Jusangjeolli+Cheonjeyeon compound is a catalog-novel pattern

low_scores.stair_climb_demand=0.2: No tuff-cone climb (Seongsan) — only Cheonjeyeon's 50-step descent + Hallim's gentle cave stairs
low_scores.tuff_cone_climb_fit=0.0: No tuff cone on this route — sister tour east route has Seongsan
low_scores.winter_visitors_seeking_floral_peak: Hydrangea peak is June; winter visitors get Camellia Hill camellia bloom but not hydrangea tunnel
low_scores.private_tour_fit=0.05: Fixed small-group itinerary preserves the operator's cave timing + lunch pairing

related_products.season_pair_east_route: jeju-hydrangea-festival-tour-east-route (existing — different POIs: Ilchul Land, Hueree Nature Park, east coast)
related_products.note: Two hydrangea tours coexist with different routes (east + southwest). User preference for east vs southwest can be inferred from POI tags.

matching_metadata.differentiation_vs_east_hydrangea_sister: Same 'hydrangea festival' product family, but **southwest garden-plus-geology emphasis**: 3 garden anchors (Hallim + Camellia Hill + Osulloc tea-fields) + 1 compound geology stop (Jusangjeolli+Cheonjeyeon) + 1 west-coast beach (Hyeopjae). The east sister covers **Hamdeok beach + Seongeup Joseon heritage + triple-UNESCO Seongsan + Ilchul Land + Hueree** — a more UNESCO-and-heritage-coded, higher-climb route. Booking matrix: guests with strong wedding-photo or hydrangea-tunnel interest + lower mobility tolerance + multi-garden preference should book southwest; guests with strong UNESCO + Joseon heritage interest + willing to climb Seongsan should book east. **Both routes share Camellia Hill peak alignment in mid-June**, so the operator can offer parallel departures for both routes simultaneously during peak hydrangea weeks.

seasonal_window.typhoon_window: August — typhoon-track season; tour reschedule policy applies

props.headlineLine1: Hydrangea, columnar cliff, and tea fields
props.headlineLine2: in one southwest Jeju summer day

hero.tagline: Beach to columnar cliff to hydrangea tunnel.

itineraryStops.name: Hyeopjae Beach
itineraryStops.description: One of Jeju's most photogenic beaches — emerald water, white sand, and Biyangdo island offshore. Easy morning warm-up before the day's main bloom stops.
highlights: Emerald turquoise water
View of Biyangdo island
White sand walking promenade
timeUsed: Brief context on west coast geology
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Opens the day with the lightest stop — sets a relaxed pace before the higher-impact hydrangea sites.

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours; managed swim season July–August

smartNotes.photo: Sunset shots with Biyangdo silhouette; turquoise color is most vivid at midday under direct sun
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along beachfront; convenience stores
smartNotes.tip: Combine with Hallim Park (5 min walk); avoid summer weekends for fewer crowds

_poi_meta.note: Operational details based on aggregated visitor info

itineraryStops.name: Hallim Park (Hydrangea Festival)
itineraryStops.description: Botanical garden complex hosting Jeju's largest hydrangea festival in early summer — multiple themed gardens, a lava cave system on-site, and varied bloom colors.
highlights: Hydrangea festival main gardens
On-site lava tube cave system
Themed garden zones
timeUsed: Photo time at peak bloom areas
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Peak hydrangea visual stop. Mid-morning timing gives best light without midday heat.

visitBasics.hours: Mar–May 09:00–18:30; Jun–Aug 09:00–19:00; Sep–Oct 09:00–18:30; Nov–Feb 09:00–17:30 (last admission 1 hour before close)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 15,000 KRW / Teen 10,000 KRW / Child 9,000 KRW (online discount: ≈13,500 / 9,000 / 8,100)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 2–3 hours of walking through 9 garden zones; mostly flat

smartNotes.photo: Hydrangea Festival (June) and pink muhly (October) are peak photo seasons; lava caves with stalactite lighting are unique
smartNotes.facilities: On-site restaurants, gift shop, cave museum
smartNotes.tip: Last entry 90 minutes before closing; closing time depends on sunset; pair with Hyeopjae Beach (5–10 min walk)

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Southwest Jeju)
itineraryStops.description: Local restaurant near Hallim or Daejeong — black pork, abalone porridge, or seasonal seafood. Group reset before afternoon stops.
highlights: Traditional Jeju cuisine
Regional restaurant
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Midday timing balances the morning bloom stops with the afternoon geology and culture stops.

visitBasics.admission: Lunch cost paid direct, ≈10,000–15,000 KRW typical (Korean local restaurants near route)

smartNotes.tip: Vegetarian options on request — notify the day before.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (lunch); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

itineraryStops.name: Jusangjeolli Cliff & Cheonjeyeon Falls
itineraryStops.description: UNESCO Geopark columnar basalt cliff at Jusangjeolli paired with the nearby three-tier Cheonjeyeon waterfall. Two adjacent UNESCO-recognized geological sites.
highlights: Hexagonal columnar basalt cliff
Three-tier waterfall in subtropical forest
UNESCO Geopark + Biosphere
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Adds geological depth and a clear UNESCO narrative anchor in the southwest part of the day.

visitBasics.admission: Jusangjeolli: Adult 2,000 / Child 1,000. Cheonjeyeon: Adult 2,500 / Child 1,350. Combined ≈4,500 KRW per adult.
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat wooden boardwalk along cliff; total walk 15–20 min

smartNotes.photo: Best with high tide and rough seas — waves smashing into the columns make dramatic shots; golden hour gives best column-color contrast
smartNotes.tip: Get there before 11am or after 4pm to avoid tour-bus crowds; combine with Jungmun Saekdal Beach (5 min) for a half-day
smartNotes.facilities: Small geological exhibition near entrance; souvenir shop

itineraryStops.name: Camellia Hill (Hydrangea Tunnel)
itineraryStops.description: Botanical garden best known for its dense hydrangea tunnel — a long path lined with massive blue, purple, and pink blooms forming a tunnel canopy.
highlights: Hydrangea tunnel walk
Camellia and rose garden zones (off-season for camellias)
Themed sculpture installations
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Second hydrangea peak — the tunnel format is visually distinct from Hallim's open garden bloom field.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 10,000 KRW / Teen 8,000 KRW / Child 7,000 KRW (approximate)
visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — gentle hill paths; mostly stroller-accessible

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance and within garden

smartNotes.photo: Hydrangea tunnel in June; pink muhly grass field in October; camellia varieties peak Nov–Apr
smartNotes.tip: Visit before 10am or after 4pm in peak season to avoid crowds; pair with Osulloc (10 min drive)
smartNotes.facilities: Indoor greenhouse café, gift shop, picnic areas

itineraryStops.name: Osulloc Tea Museum & Tea Fields
itineraryStops.description: Endless green tea fields layered against Hallasan in the distance, plus the Osulloc tea museum and tea house. Closes the day with a tea culture context and relaxed pacing.
highlights: Vast green tea field photo zones
Tea museum exhibits
Green tea ice cream and snacks at tea house
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Closes the day calmly with a culture-tea anchor that contrasts with the morning bloom intensity.

visitBasics.admission: Free for museum; tea/desserts in café paid; Innisfree workshops paid separately
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat museum and stroller-friendly tea fields; well-maintained paths

smartNotes.photo: Tea fields with tour-cup shaped museum building; outdoor seating area; cherry blossoms peak shoulder season
smartNotes.tip: Arrive before 10:30 to beat the tour-bus rush; allow 1.5–2 hours including café time and tea field walk
smartNotes.facilities: Café (must-try green tea ice cream), gift shop, Innisfree Jeju House next door, observatory deck overlooking fields

routeShapeIntro.title: How this summer day moves
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Beach warm-up, hydrangea festival, lunch reset, then UNESCO geology, hydrangea tunnel, and tea fields. 08:30 to 17:30.

items.label: Beach as Light Opener
items.detail: Hyeopjae as a low-intensity start lets the group settle before higher-attention bloom and geology sites.

items.label: Two Hydrangea Stops, Different Formats
items.detail: Hallim festival garden and Camellia tunnel give complementary compositions of the same flower — bookend morning and afternoon.

items.label: Tea to Close
items.detail: Osulloc closes calmly — culture and rest after the visual intensity of bloom and falls.

items.label: Hydrangea Window Late May - mid-July
items.detail: Hydrangea blooms appear from late May, peak mid-June, and fade by mid-July. Outside this window the bloom stops lose their visual draw.

items.label: Outside Window, Pick SW-Small
items.detail: If your dates fall outside hydrangea season, our West & South year-round route covers the same region with year-round POIs (Hallasan, Osulloc, Aewol).

items.label: Camellia Hill is Not Camellia Season
items.detail: Despite the name, this tour visits Camellia Hill specifically for hydrangea — actual camellias bloom in winter.

items.label: 9 Hours Is Long
items.detail: Six diverse stops in 9 hours — pace is fuller than east-small. Build energy with steady hydration.

items.label: Some Stops Have Admission
items.detail: Hallim Park (15,000 KRW) and Camellia Hill (10,000 KRW) admissions are paid on-site, not bundled into the tour price. Budget ≈30,000 KRW per person across all admissions (Jusangjeolli 2,000 + Cheonjeyeon 2,500 round it out).

items.label: Heat Can Be Real
items.detail: June Jeju can hit 30°C+ at midday. Bring sun protection — most stops are outdoor.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hotel lobby pickup in Jeju City area
content: Pickup from your hotel lobby between 08:00-08:30
Return drop-off by approximately 17:30-18:00
Hotel lobby pickup within Jeju City is free. Pickup from Seogwipo, Aewol, or Seongsan adds 70,000 KRW due to extra driving distance.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
content: Comfortable walking shoes (multiple garden walks)
Sun protection — hat, sunscreen, sunglasses
Light layer for AC vehicle and tea house
Cash or card for lunch and admission

practicalAccordionItems.title: Hydrangea season window
content: Tour season: June 1 - July 15 (varies ±5 days year to year)
Peak window: June 25 - July 5 (best photographic conditions)
We update the booking page with year-specific bloom forecast in early May
If your travel dates fall outside the window, consider our West & South year-round route instead

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather policy
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Light rain operates; heavy rain reschedules
content: Light rain: tour operates (hydrangea actually photographs well in soft rain)
Heavy rain: free rescheduling within season window
Outside-window cancellations follow the booking platform's standard terms — see the booking confirmation for specifics.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
content: Hotel pickup and drop-off (Jeju City)
Air-conditioned van transport
Licensed English-speaking guide
Major admissions (Hallim Park, Camellia Hill, Jusangjeolli, Cheonjeyeon) are paid on-site, not bundled into the tour price
Lunch separate from base price

today.label: Summer weather guidance
today.temp: Daytime ≈ 26–30 °C in hydrangea season (late May to mid-July, peak mid-June)

tomorrow.label: Live forecast available at the operator's booking page

seasonalVariations.name: Peak (Jun 25 - Jul 5)
seasonalVariations.description: Peak hydrangea bloom across all gardens — best photographic conditions

seasonalVariations.name: Late Bloom (Jul 6-15)
seasonalVariations.description: Hydrangeas still strong but starting to fade. Less crowded.

seasonalVariations.name: Outside window (Jul 16 - May 31)
seasonalVariations.description: Out of season — our West & South year-round route recommended

bookingSupportSteps.title: Weather Check-in
bookingSupportSteps.detail: We monitor forecast and reach out if rebooking is recommended

bookingSupportSteps.title: Vehicle & Guide Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Pickup time, vehicle plate, and guide name via WhatsApp

staticQuestions.question: Is this good for first-time visitors to Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes, if you're visiting during hydrangea season (late May to mid-July, peak mid-June). Outside that window, our West & South year-round route covers the same region with year-round POIs.

staticQuestions.question: When exactly is hydrangea season?
staticQuestions.answer: Approximately late May to mid-July (peak mid-June), with peak typically in mid-June. We update the booking page with year-specific forecast in early May.

staticQuestions.question: What if it rains?
staticQuestions.answer: Light rain — we operate (hydrangea actually photographs beautifully in soft rain). Heavy rain — we offer free rescheduling within the season window.

staticQuestions.question: Why two hydrangea stops?
staticQuestions.answer: Hallim Park is an open festival garden; Camellia Hill is a tunnel format. Same flower, complementary compositions.

staticQuestions.question: Is this suitable for children?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes, age 6+. Walking is moderate at most stops; hydrangea gardens are family-friendly.

staticQuestions.question: Is this ok for seniors?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes, mostly easy walking. Cheonjeyeon waterfall path has some stairs — can be skipped while group views Jusangjeolli.

staticQuestions.question: Where do you pick up?
staticQuestions.answer: Hotel lobby pickup within Jeju City is free. Pickup from Seogwipo, Aewol, or Seongsan adds 70,000 KRW due to extra driving distance.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: Lunch cost is separate. Expect 12,000-18,000 KRW per person.

staticQuestions.question: Are admission fees included?
staticQuestions.answer: Admissions are not bundled by default. Budget ≈30,000 KRW per person: Hallim Park 15,000, Camellia Hill 10,000, Jusangjeolli 2,000, Cheonjeyeon 2,500, Osulloc free.

staticQuestions.question: Is Osulloc included?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — museum entry is free. The tea house is separate (optional purchase).

staticQuestions.question: What's the difference vs our West & South year-round route?
staticQuestions.answer: Different POIs and seasonality. Our West & South year-round route is year-round and visits Hallasan 1100, Osulloc, and Aewol. This tour is summer-only and adds Hyeopjae, Hallim, Jusangjeolli, Cheonjeyeon, Camellia Hill while keeping Osulloc as the closing stop.

props.note: checkout_tour_id resolves at runtime from Supabase / env; not part of static JSONB.

pickup_dropoff.notes: Free pickup in Jeju City. Outside-city pickup adds ₩70,000. Hydrangea stops only in bloom late May to mid-July (peak mid-June); check before booking.

fix_passes.v12: P0+P1 — pickup_dropoff Schema A unification, routeFlowStops type-based, competing_products → internal slugs, anchor_poi_keys aligned
fix_passes.v13: P1.5 — pickup_dropoff Schema A.2 → A.1; practicalAccordionItems / routePhases / seasonalVariations unified to V4 canonical (no Tailwind in data)
fix_passes.v14: P2 — _publication uniform; duration formatting standardized; glanceItems Duration added where missing
fix_passes.v15: P3 round 1 — SEO trim. seo.pageTitle rewritten to ≤65 chars (was 14/25 over 70); seo.metaDescription rewritten to ≤168 chars (was 25/25 over 170). primaryKeywords and ogImage unchanged. Hero/OG images, POI verification, review imports, and routePhases enrichment deferred to later P3 rounds.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.

v17_batch5_enhancement.description: Batch 5 deep enhancement — descriptions raised to 700-1300+ chars; KB-backed metadata on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated with verified-fact 0-1 float scores. Time-weighted indoor_ratio per Fix 1 methodology.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale. Indoor ratio computed time-weighted with Fix 1 methodology.

unesco_fit.reasoning: Verified — NO direct UNESCO World Heritage stops on this tour. Hallim Park caves are Korean National Monument (No. 236), NOT UNESCO. Jusangjeolli is Korean Natural Monument (No. 443), NOT UNESCO. Cheonjeyeon, Hyeopjae, Camellia Hill, Osulloc are non-UNESCO. Mechanical 1.0 was overstated. 0.45 reflects strong national-heritage anchors but no direct UNESCO.

walking_level.reasoning: Verified: Hyeopjae photo-stop (minimal), Hallim 1.5-3 km flat with cave ducking, Jusangjeolli 0.5 km boardwalk, Cheonjeyeon ~150 stairs to mid falls, Camellia Hill ~1.5 km flat, Osulloc 0.5 km. Total ~5 km mostly flat with one stair section. 0.50 calibrated.

scenic_level.reasoning: Verified: turquoise beach + 9-area botanical garden + 2 lava-tube caves + columnar basalt cliff + three-tier waterfall + camellia hydrangea tunnel + green tea fields. 7 distinct landscapes — among catalog's most diverse. 0.90 calibrated.

photo_level.reasoning: Verified: 6+ hero shots — Hyeopjae beach + Hallim caves/folk village + Jusangjeolli columns + Cheonjeyeon nymphs + Camellia hydrangea tunnel + Osulloc fields. Catalog-leading photo density. 0.95.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: Verified: Hyeopjae Top 3 Jeju beach + Hallim Park 1.2M annual visitors + Jusangjeolli Natural Monument 443 + Cheonjeyeon (only 3-tier falls on Jeju) + Osulloc (Jeju's most famous tea brand). 5/6 internationally iconic. 0.85.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: CORRECTION using time-weighted method (Fix 1 methodology): 108 indoor / 405 total = 0.27 with lunch; 60/345 = 0.17 without. Catalog value 0.20 represents both. Mechanical 0.4 overstated.

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: VERIFIED 0.85 — hydrangea bloom timing weather-dependent; Hyeopjae + Jusangjeolli + Camellia Hill + Osulloc all primarily outdoor. Hallim caves provide partial weather refuge.

rain_fit.reasoning: Hallim caves (~600m total) + Osulloc indoor museum + restaurant lunch provide refuge. But majority of stops outdoor. 0.55 calibrated.

first_time_fit.reasoning: Verified: covers 7 distinct Jeju landscapes — among the most representative single-day southwest summaries. Season-specific to summer for hydrangea. 0.85.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified fees: Hyeopjae free + Hallim ₩11,000 + Jusangjeolli ₩2,000 + Cheonjeyeon ₩2,500 + Camellia Hill ₩9,000 + Osulloc free = ~₩24,500/adult. Higher than other Jeju tours due to multiple paid stops. 0.70.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Hallim Safari Bird Garden + caves + folk village + Hyeopjae Beach + Cheonjeyeon ravine. Strong family appeal. 0.85.

senior_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — most stops senior-accessible (Hallim flat + Hyeopjae photo-stop + Osulloc flat); Cheonjeyeon stairs are the one challenge. 0.65.

stroller_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Hallim caves require ducking; otherwise mostly stroller-friendly. 0.65 calibrated for the cave caveat.

beach_fit.reasoning: UPGRADED — Hyeopjae IS the beach component (Top 3 Jeju). Photo-stop format limits beach time. 0.55.

culture_level.reasoning: Verified: Hallim Folk Village + Cheonjeyeon Seven Nymphs legend + Osulloc tea heritage. 3/6 cultural anchors. 0.55.

nature_fit.reasoning: Verified: beach + botanical garden + caves + columnar cliff + waterfall + flower park + tea fields. Catalog-leading nature density. 0.90.

geological_fit.reasoning: Verified: Hyeopjaegul + Ssangyonggul caves (lava+limestone) + Jusangjeolli columnar joints. 2 distinct geological landmarks. 0.85.

_extended_dimensions_documented_only.note: These dimensions (snow_fit, active_fit, geological_fit, experiential_fit) were assessed during the v17 verified-fact audit but are NOT yet part of the standard 68-key matching_profile. They are preserved here as documentation for future schema extension.
